event: 25 years old
female
gave birth to a baby girl
pregnancy had been uncomplicated
spontaneous vaginal delivery with an episiotomy
complained of severe pain
shortness of breath of sudden onset
worsening pain over her right thigh
arrived at the emergency department
drowsy
tachypneic
oxygen saturation of 56% on room air
received high-flow oxygen supply
tachycardia with a pulse was 121 bpm
afebrile at 36 °C
unrecordable blood pressure
hypotensive with a blood pressure of 56/30 mmHg
started noradrenaline infusion
received a stat dose of IV heparin 5000 units
received IV amoxicillin-clavulanate stat dose
transferred to a tertiary hospital
electively intubated for severe metabolic and lactic acidosis
given a total of 120 mL/kg (6 L) of crystalloid
persistently hypotensive post-intubation
started on IV meropenem in combination with IV clindamycin and IV vancomycin
high vaginal swab for culture and sensitivity
started CVVH due to severe lactic acidosis and toxin removal
grossly swollen right thigh
extensive blistering ecchymotic patches over her right thigh extending to her right buttock
diagnosed with necrotizing fasciitis of the right thigh with septicemic shock complicated by acute kidney injury, rhabdomyolysis, coagulopathy with thrombocytopenia and ischemic hepatitis
intravenous immunoglobulin given for toxin neutralization
skin lesion spread over her bilateral upper and lower limbs
blistering of the bilateral lower limbs
blistering of the bilateral upper limbs
bluish discoloration of the skin lesion
produced blistering of the bilateral lower limbs that extended to her right lower abdomen
similar lesion was also noted on her right upper limb that extended to her right elbow
Gram positive cocci in chains on staining
grew S. pyogenes
multiple blood cultures revealed Gram positive cocci in chains on staining
blood culture taken at the district hospital prior to the transfer was positive for group A beta hemolytic streptococcus
diagnosed with group A streptococcal toxic shock syndrome with necrotizing fasciitis
IV clindamycin restarted for toxin suppression
high dose of IV crystalline penicillin G given every four hours
CVVH continued for toxin removal
surgical intervention was not feasible
succumbed to death
cause of death determined to be septic shock with tissue necrosis and toxic shock syndrome secondary to S. pyogenes
